title: De-identification design guidance (Safe Harbor / Expert Determination)
context: Adopters of the CDE set who want to share data beyond their registry need de-identification guidance. The project provides de-identification *design* guidance so adopters de-identify their own data in their own custody — the project never receives identifiable data to de-identify. This is HIGH risk-tier: guidance on HIPAA Safe Harbor and Expert Determination methods is informational and must be primary-source-cited and bioethics/legal-reviewed. The CDE minimal core is already de-identified-by-design, so this document complements it with guidance on optional module fields. This is unscheduled backlog.
objective: Author de-identification design guidance (`compliance/deid-guidance.md`) covering: (1) HIPAA §164.514(b) Safe Harbor method — the 18 identifier categories to remove — and Expert Determination method with primary-source citations; (2) how the minimal-core CDE set is de-identified-by-design and which optional module fields require additional de-identification before sharing; (3) field-level notes on re-identification risk in the optional modules; and (4) clear 'guidance only — we never receive identifiable data' and 'informational, not legal advice' notices throughout.
acceptanceCriteria: HIPAA §164.514(b) Safe Harbor method (18 identifier categories) is described with primary-source citation.
HIPAA §164.514(b) Expert Determination method is described with primary-source citation.
Document explains how the minimal-core CDE fields are de-identified-by-design and which optional module fields introduce re-identification risk.
Field-level notes in the optional modules are referenced, stating which fields need de-identification before sharing.
Document carries 'Guidance only — this project never receives identifiable data to de-identify' and 'Informational — not legal advice; consult your IRB/REC, DPO, and counsel' notices on every page.
Bioethics/legal reviewer sign-off recorded, version-scoped, before publication.
output: A `compliance/deid-guidance.md` document with primary-source-cited Safe Harbor and Expert Determination guidance, field-level notes, and 'not legal advice' notices, committed via PR with bioethics/legal reviewer sign-off.
